Returns the created DebugToken resource with the token value.

data.current_description: Generates a challenge that protects the integrity of an immediately following call to ExchangeAppAttestAttestation or ExchangeAppAttestAssertion. A challenge should not be reused for multiple calls.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate App Attest challenge for integrity protection
data.description_suggestion: Generates a cryptographic challenge that must be used in the immediately following ExchangeAppAttestAttestation or ExchangeAppAttestAssertion call to protect the integrity of the attestation process. The returned challenge is single-use and must not be reused across multiple attestation requests. Returns a challenge token that the client application must include in the subsequent App Attest exchange operation.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity about what the challenge is used for (integrity protection), what the operation returns, and the consequences of challenge reuse. Added summary and expanded description to clarify the challenge's purpose, single-use nature, and return value for better AI agent understanding of the attestation workflow.

message: Operation 'firebaseappcheck.projects.apps.generatePlayIntegrityChallenge' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Missing summary entirely. data.current_description: Generates a challenge that protects the integrity of an immediately following integrity verdict request to the Play Integrity API. The next call to ExchangePlayIntegrityToken using the resulting integrity token will verify the presence and validity of the challenge. A challenge should not be reused for multiple calls.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate Play Integrity challenge for token verification
data.description_suggestion: Generates a cryptographic challenge that must be included in the Play Integrity API request to protect the integrity of the verdict process. The challenge is verified when the resulting integrity token is exchanged via ExchangePlayIntegrityToken. The returned challenge is single-use and must not be reused across multiple integrity requests. Returns a challenge token that the client application must include in the Play Integrity API call.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is verbose and unclear about what is returned and how the challenge flows through the system. Added concise summary and clarified description to explain the challenge's purpose, single-use requirement, and return value for better AI agent understanding of the Play Integrity workflow.

data.current_description: Deletes the specified DebugToken. A deleted debug token cannot be used to exchange for an App Check token. Use this method when you suspect the secret `token` has been compromised or when you no longer need the debug token.
data.summary_suggestion: Delete a debug token
data.description_suggestion: Deletes the specified debug token, preventing it from being used to exchange for App Check tokens. After deletion, any attempts to use the debug token will fail. Use this operation when the debug token's secret has been compromised, when development/testing is complete, or when the token is no longer needed. Returns an empty response on successful deletion.

data.current_description: Returns a public JWK set as specified by [RFC 7517](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7517) that can be used to verify App Check tokens. Exactly one of the public keys in the returned set will successfully validate any App Check token that is currently valid.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve public JWK set for App Check token verification
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the public JSON Web Key (JWK) set that can be used to verify the signature of App Check tokens. The returned set conforms to RFC 7517 and contains the public keys needed for token validation. Exactly one key in the set will successfully validate any currently valid App Check token. Backend services should use this endpoint to obtain the keys needed for verifying App Check tokens presented by client applications.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks context about the operation's purpose (backend token verification) and who should use it. data.current_description: Updates the RecaptchaV3Config for the specified app. While this configuration is incomplete or invalid, the app will be unable to exchange reCAPTCHA V3 tokens for App Check tokens. For security reasons, the `site_secret` field is never populated in the response.
data.summary_suggestion: Update reCAPTCHA v3 configuration for app
data.description_suggestion: Updates the reCAPTCHA v3 configuration for the specified app, including the site secret and other settings. The configuration must be complete and valid for the app to successfully exchange reCAPTCHA v3 tokens for App Check tokens. Incomplete or invalid configurations will prevent token exchange. For security reasons, the `site_secret` field is never included in the response, even though it was updated. Returns the updated RecaptchaV3Config object (without the secret).
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity about what fields can be updated, the consequences of invalid configuration, and what is returned. Added summary and expanded description to clarify the update scope, validation requirements, security considerations, and return value for better AI agent understanding.
